en.extract:
The Timor heleia (Heleia muelleri), also known as the spot-breasted white-eye or spot-breasted heleia, is a species of bird in the white-eye family Zosteropidae. It is found on Timor island. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest. It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''Timor heleia''' ('''''Heleia muelleri'''''), also known as the '''spot-breasted white-eye''' or '''spot-breasted heleia''',<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/ioc-lists/master-list-2/ |title=Master Lists: v15.1 |website=[[IOC World Bird List]] |publisher=[[International Ornithologists' Union]] |access-date=13 March 2025}}</ref> is a species of [[bird]] in the white-eye family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is found on [[Timor]] island. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
